data.source: Online self-administered survey distributed via social media platforms
data.wave_or_subset: Data collected in May 2020 during the national COVID-19 lockdown in Jordan
data.sample_size: 381 completed questionnaires (after excluding 16 incomplete out of 397 collected)
data.access_details: Dataset available from the authors upon reasonable request
data.notes: Convenience sampling; primarily undergraduate respondents; cross-sectional design

method.description: A cross-sectional online questionnaire incorporating the 10-item Kessler Psychological Distress Scale (K10) and additional items on coping, medication use, motivation for distance learning, and pandemic concerns was administered to Jordanian university students. After piloting, the survey link was disseminated through social media in May 2020. Completed responses were analyzed in SPSS 23 using descriptive statistics, Wilcoxon Rank-Sum test, Spearman’s rank correlation, and multinomial logistic regression to examine factors associated with psychological distress severity.
steps: Design questionnaire including K10 and COVID-19-related items
Pilot the instrument on 10 students; refine as needed
Disseminate survey link via social media platforms to university students (May 2020)
Compute K10 scores and categorize distress levels (no, mild, moderate, severe)

Analyze data in SPSS v23: descriptive stats, Wilcoxon Rank-Sum test (gender differences), Spearman correlation (age vs distress), multinomial logistic regression (factors associated with distress levels)
Interpret statistical outputs and draw conclusions about psychological distress and associated factors
method.models: Wilcoxon Rank-Sum test; Spearman’s rank correlation; Multinomial logistic regression
method.outcome_variable: Psychological distress score (K10 total) and categorized distress severity
method.independent_variables: Age, gender, motivation for distance learning, coping strategies, pandemic concerns
method.control_variables: Not explicitly detailed; regression models included age, gender, and other survey variables as predictors

results.summary: The mean K10 distress score was 34.2, with 69.5% of students classified as experiencing severe psychological distress. Distress scores were inversely related to age (younger students reported higher distress). Gender was not significantly associated with overall K10 scores, although females showed higher odds of mild and severe distress categories. A strong inverse association existed between severe distress and motivation for distance learning: more distressed students reported little or no motivation. The most common coping strategy was increased social media use (70.6%), and 12.9% of respondents used medication, mainly sedative-hypnotics, to cope.

numerical_results.outcome_name: Spearman correlation between age and K10 score
Odds ratio: severe distress vs motivation for distance learning
Odds ratio: female vs male severe psychological distress

metadata.original_paper_title: Assessing Psychological Distress among University Students in Jordan during the COVID-19 Pandemic
